title: seccomp: allow all futex_* syscalls
body:
## What type of PR is this?
/kind feature

## What this PR does / why we need it:
Moves futex_* syscalls from the denied syscall list to the allowed list in the default seccomp profile.

This aligns with the default seccomp profile in docker and containerd when it comes to futex handling.

These syscalls are used for thread synchronization and waiting on multiple futexes and is particularly beneficial for workloads with large numbers of lightweight threads, such as telco packet processing and event handlers.

comments.body:
I am not aware of specific security reason why futex calls are blocked here. Looking at the docker profile they seem to be allowed https://github.com/moby/profiles/blob/935f56cec625bc2cb1b236cab4def073e5a39c8c/seccomp/default_linux.go#L129-L134

So I think enabling all futex calls should be fine.

cc @giuseppe

> I am not aware of specific security reason why futex calls are blocked here. Looking at the docker profile they seem to be allowed https://github.com/moby/profiles/blob/935f56cec625bc2cb1b236cab4def073e5a39c8c/seccomp/default_linux.go#L129-L134
> 
> So I think enabling all futex calls should be fine.
> 
> cc @giuseppe

yes, I think these syscalls are safe.

@KhalidElbashir can you enable the other futex syscalls?
Thanks @giuseppe, I have pushed a commit to allow other futex_* syscall aligning with docker and containerd default profiles.
Mind squashing the commits into one? I don't see value in merging these as two separate ones.

> Mind squashing the commits into one? I don't see value in merging these as two separate ones.

Done. @Luap99

@Luap99 @giuseppe few questions, 
will we see these changes in the next release? 
Release is going to be cut from main branch or we need to backport this to some release branch?
And when is the next release planned?
This will go out as part of Podman 6 in May, no backports needed for that.

> This will go out as part of Podman 6 in May, no backports needed for that.

Hi @Luap99, I see that v0.68.0 is released with this change. What's the next step, will CRI-O pick this up independently or can we submit a PR to bump it?
cri-o is not maintained by our team, you will need to ask them directly.
